name: Robinhood
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Robinhood's design language — the retail-investing brand built on a near-black canvas (#0a0c0d) with a signature glow-green voltage (#00C805) reserved exclusively for market-up and primary CTAs, a strict Capsule sans family at every tier, and large tabular-figure mono numerals for portfolio values. The brand reads as 'serious trading platform for the millennial investor' — confident, dark-first, data-dense.

design_language.motion_signature: Signature is the portfolio chart line drawing in at 800ms cubic-bezier on first paint; price ticks animate at 200ms with green/red flash on direction change; tabular-figure number transitions use slot-machine ticks for portfolio value updates (~150ms per digit).

trademark_signals: Robinhood Green (#00C805) — the signature electric green used for market-up indicators, primary CTAs, and the brand mark — never substituted with a darker emerald or a brighter lime
Near-black canvas (#0a0c0d) — deliberately not pure black, deliberately not navy — a warm-tinted near-black that IS the brand's trading-platform chrome
Tabular-figure mono numerals on every portfolio value, every price, every percent change — the brand's quiet financial-data signal
Capsule sans (a Robinhood custom face descended from Inter) at every tier — refuses any serif, refuses any other sans family
Portfolio chart with green/red line — the chart line color flips to red on negative-percent days, green on positive, with the gradient fill below the line
ai_slop_avoided: Don't substitute the Robinhood Green (#00C805) with a darker emerald or a brighter lime. The electric green IS the brand voltage
Don't paint the canvas pure black or navy. The warm-tinted near-black #0a0c0d is the brand chrome
Don't use proportional figures for portfolio values. Tabular figures are non-negotiable for financial UI
Don't substitute Capsule sans with Helvetica or Inter. The Capsule face is the brand's typographic signature
Don't add a third chromatic accent. Green for up + red for down + white for chrome — three colors only
